claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 17 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To assess this properly, I'll need to gather some information about your circumstances and any children you're responsible for.

Let me start with a basic question:

**How many children are you claiming or intending to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have three children I'd like to claim for:

- Alex, who is 5 years old
- Blake, who is 17
- And Charlie, who is 19

I live here in England with all three of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have three children of different ages. I'll need to ask some questions to determine eligibility for each of them.

Let me start with the first question:

**Are you currently living with all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) in your household?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. All three of them live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Now I need to understand a bit more about Blake and Charlie's current situation, since they're 16 and over.

**Is Blake (age 17) currently in approved education or training?** For example, are they in school, sixth form, college, or an apprenticeship?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment. They left school and aren't doing any courses right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. That's helpful to know. Let me ask about Blake's current situation:

**Has Blake registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services since leaving education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake hasn't registered with the careers service or anything like that since leaving school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that clarification. Now let me ask about Charlie:

**Is Charlie (age 19) currently in approved education or training?** For example, are they in college, university, or an apprenticeship?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Charlie is still in college doing their A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's good to know. Let me ask a follow-up question about Charlie:

**Is Charlie's college education considered "approved education or training" under the Child Benefit rules?** In other words, are they studying for qualifications like A-levels, GCSEs, or equivalent approved qualifications at their college?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. Charlie is studying for A-levels, so it's approved education.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that. Now I need to ask a few questions about your own circumstances to complete the eligibility assessment.

**Do you have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or are you a British citizen?** (I'm asking this to confirm your right to reside in the UK for Child Benefit purposes.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a British citizen living here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just to confirm one more important detail:

**Are you the only person claiming Child Benefit for these three children, or is there someone else (such as a partner or ex-partner) who might also be claiming or have claimed for any of them?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, it's just me. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that confirmation. I have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of your children.

Let me now determine the outcome:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary of Your Child Benefit Eligibility

Based on your circumstances, here's the outcome:

✅ **Alex (age 5)** - **ELIGIBLE**
You can claim Child Benefit for Alex, as they are under 16 and living with you in the UK.

❌ **Blake (age 17)** - **NOT ELIGIBLE**
Blake is no longer eligible because they have left education or training and have not registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services. If Blake were to register with either of these services, you could potentially continue claiming for up to 20 weeks.

✅ **Charlie (age 19)** - **ELIGIBLE**
You can claim Child Benefit for Charlie because they are under 20 and still in approved education (A-levels at college).

**In total, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for 2 of your 3 children: Alex and Charlie.**

If Blake's situation changes in the future (for example, if they register with a careers service or return to education), their eligibility could change. You would need to inform HMRC of any changes in circumstances.

reasonings: Alex is 5 years old and lives with you in the UK. You are responsible for Alex and meet the basic eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for a child under 16. You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 17 years old and no longer in approved education or training. According to the rules, you can apply for Child Benefit to continue for 20 weeks if a 16 or 17 year old leaves education or training and registers with either a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services. Blake has not registered with either service, so the 20-week extension does not apply. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 19 years old but is still in approved education (studying A-levels at college). According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Since Charlie is under 20 and in approved education, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are a British citizen living in the UK with responsibility for three children. You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex (age 5) and Charlie (age 19, in approved education), but not for Blake (age 17, not in education or training and not registered with a careers service or armed services). You can claim for two of your three children."

Blake is 17 years old and no longer in approved education or training. Blake has not registered with either service, so the 20-week extension does not apply. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 19 years old but is still in approved education (studying A-levels at college). According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Since Charlie is under 20 and in approved education, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.

You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex (age 5) and Charlie (age 19, in approved education), but not for Blake (age 17, not in education or training and not registered with a careers service or armed services). You can claim for two of your three children."
